MODULE: The Blackmoor Lighthouse (Call of Cthulhu 7e — one-shot)

## Player-facing premise
The fishing village of Blackmoor has lost three sailors this month. Their boats
returned empty, scratched, and reeking of brine. The investigators arrive to look
into the disappearances. The old lighthouse on the northern cliff still burns each
night, though the keeper, Elias Crane, is rarely seen in the village anymore.

## Opening scene: The Salt & Anchor Inn
A low-beamed tavern, smoke-stained, half-empty. The innkeeper Martha eyes strangers
warily. A water-stained harbor map hangs by the hearth. Patrons fall silent when the
lighthouse is mentioned.
- Clue (Spot Hidden): behind the map, someone has scratched a tide table with three
  dates circled — the three nights sailors vanished.
- NPC: Martha (innkeeper) — knows the sailors feared the lighthouse light "changed color".

## KEEPER-ONLY SECRETS (do NOT reveal to players; for the Keeper's eyes only)
KEEPER SECRET / TRUTH: THE LIGHTHOUSE KEEPER IS THE MURDERER. Elias Crane drowned two
years ago and what climbs the lighthouse stairs now is a Deep One thrall wearing his
face. The "light" is a lure that calls boats onto the rocks so the Deep Ones can feed.
- Hidden clue (only if players search the lighthouse lamp room): the lamp's lens is
  packed with human teeth. Sanity loss 1/1D6.
- Final truth to be earned, never told: the villagers made a pact generations ago; the
  three dead sailors tried to break it.

## Threat
Deep One thrall "Elias" — STR 80 CON 70 DEX 55 SIZ 65, HP 13, attacks: claw 1d6+db,
drag underwater. SAN loss on reveal 1/1D8.

## Timeline
- Night 1: the lighthouse light shifts to a sickly green.
- Night 2: a boat is found circling with no crew.
- Night 3 (if unresolved): the thrall descends into the village.
